goals.description: Send the direct text to Mina and the cohort right now, explicitly stating that skipping the club is a non-negotiable boundary for our team's future reliability, framing it as me valuing us over convenience.
goals.rationale: Mina is leaving in minutes. My affiliation axis screams that if I don't address this *now*, they'll assume I ghosted them or dropped them, which destroys more trust than missing the party ever could. I need to be the rude one here—cutting through the politeness to protect the relationship. If I wait until tomorrow, I look like a liar; sending it now makes me look like someone who values us over convenience.

goals.description: Immediately launch into the study block after hitting send, ignoring all urges to scroll or delay, and lock the laptop by midnight so I can finally sleep.
goals.rationale: I've just secured the boundary with the friends (g1). Now I can stop feeling guilty about the club night because I've prioritized my own survival. My impulsive nature means I might want to keep scrolling or delaying, but I have to force a shutdown ritual right after the text lands. If I don't get this done before midnight, I wake up exhausted, which hurts my health and makes me less capable of being present for my friends later anyway.

agent.last_reasoning: Mina leaves in minutes; affiliation axis demands I send this *now* to prevent trust destruction. Being the 'rude' one cuts through politeness and proves I value the relationship above convenience. Once sent, I'll immediately launch into study block to lock down my health before midnight.

goals.description: Directly tell Mina and the cohort that I can't make it to Echo tonight, explicitly stating my need for sleep to avoid looking flaky or untrustworthy.
goals.rationale: My affiliation axis demands I maintain high trust with my close group. Even though I'm impulsive and want to skip studying, skipping the social event entirely without a clear boundary would signal avoidance. I need to renegotiate this immediately so they know I value them enough to be honest, rather than ghosting.

goals.description: Do exactly 45 minutes of targeted midterm review right now, then stop. No more scrolling, no more 'just one more minute' loops.
goals.rationale: I have a short planning horizon and procrastination bias, but I also care about health and avoiding guilt. Since I've already decided to prioritize the conversation over the full study session, I'll only do enough to feel slightly productive before I collapse into bed. This keeps the task manageable without burning out my physical stamina.

goals.description: Put the phone in another room and go to sleep immediately after saying no.
goals.rationale: My stress is already moderate and my sleep debt is piling up. Scrolling is just delaying the inevitable exhaustion. Once the conflict is resolved (goal g1), I need to secure my rest because my energy reserves are low; there's no point in worrying about tomorrow if I'm not functioning well tonight.

desires: Not look like I'm avoiding them or being untrustworthy to Mina and the cohort right now, even though I know I have to say no.

Actually get some sleep tonight instead of scrolling on my phone worrying about what they think.
